title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud infrastructure by exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines. This can lead to data encryption, operational disruptions, financial losses, and potential data exfiltration.
recommended_actions: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) and strict access controls for CI/CD pipelines and cloud environments.
Conduct an immediate audit of CI/CD pipelines to identify and remediate potential vulnerabilities or misconfigurations.
Deploy advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and monitor for unusual activity in cloud environments.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: The Highland Software Custom Role Manager plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege...
description: The Highland Software Custom Role Manager plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in versions up to and including 1.0.0. This is due to insufficient authorization checks in the hscrm_save_user_roles() function, which is hooked to the personal_options_update action accessible by any authenticated user. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access or higher, to potentially modify user roles via the profile update form.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7106.

title:
Successful exploitation of the race condition vulnerability could allow

description:

an attacker to trigger a kernel heap overflow, potentially leading to local privilege
escalation and granting system-level access to the affected software.

title: A weakness has been identified in Tenda F456 1.0.0.5. This affects the function fromAdvSetWan of...
description: A weakness has been identified in Tenda F456 1.0.0.5. This affects the function fromAdvSetWan of the file /goform/AdvSetWan of the component httpd. This manipulation of the argument wanmode causes buffer overflow. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified buffer overflow vulnerability in Tenda F456 1.0.0.5 could allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or disruption of services. The public availability of the exploit increases the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available firmware updates or patches from Tenda to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the device's administrative interface by enabling firewall rules and disabling remote management if not required.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda F456 1.0.0.5. The impacted element is the function...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda F456 1.0.0.5. The impacted element is the function fromSetIpBind of the file /goform/SetIpBind of the component httpd. The manipulation of the argument page results in buffer overflow. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Tenda F456 1.0.0.5 allows for remote exploitation via a buffer overflow in the fromSetIpBind function, potentially leading to unauthorized access, system compromise, or denial of service. The public availability of the exploit increases the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available firmware updates or patches provided by Tenda to address CVE-2026-7078.
Implement network-level protections such as firewalls or intrusion prevention systems (IPS) to monitor and block suspicious traffic targeting the /goform/SetIpBind endpoint.
If no patch is available, consider isolating affected devices from critical networks and disabling remote access to the router's management interface.

title: A vulnerability was found in itsourcecode Construction Management System 1.0. This issue affects...
description: A vulnerability was found in itsourcecode Construction Management System 1.0. This issue affects some unknown processing of the file /locations.php. Performing a manipulation of the argument address results in sql injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to exploit SQL injection through the 'address' parameter in /locations.php, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data, database manipulation, or full system compromise.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) using the software
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for the 'address' parameter in /locations.php to prevent SQL injection.
Update the Construction Management System to the latest patched version if available or contact the vendor for a security patch.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts as a temporary mitigation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform SQL injection via the 'Username' parameter in the /api/login.php file, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or database manipulation.
recommended_actions: Immediately patch the application by updating to a fixed version or applying input sanitization and parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.
Conduct a thorough security audit of the application to identify and address similar vulnerabilities.
Monitor for unusual activity in the database and implement web application firewalls (WAF) to detect and block malicious requests.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in Intina47 context-sync up to 2.0.0. This affects an...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Intina47 context-sync up to 2.0.0. This affects an unknown part of the file src/git-integration.ts of the component Git Integration. Such manipulation leads to os command injection. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Intina47 context-sync up to 2.0.0 allows for OS command injection via the Git Integration component. If exploited, attackers could execute arbitrary commands on the host system, potentially leading to data exfiltration, system compromise, or further lateral movement within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade Intina47 context-sync to a version newer than 2.0.0, if available, or apply any patches provided by the vendor.
Restrict access to the affected component by implementing network-level controls, such as firewalls or IP whitelisting, to limit exposure to potential attackers.
Conduct a thorough review of system logs for any signs of exploitation and monitor for unusual activity, particularly around the Git Integration component.

The impacted element is the function SimulationIPCClient.send_command of the file backend/app/services/simulation_ipc.py of the component Inter-Process Communication. Such manipulation leads to command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. title: A security flaw has been discovered in D-Link DIR-825 up to 3.00b32. This impacts the function...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in D-Link DIR-825 up to 3.00b32. This impacts the function AddPortMapping of the file upnpsoap.c of the component miniupnpd. Performing a manipulation of the argument NewPortMappingDescription results in buffer overflow. The attack needs to be approached within the local network. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. This vulnerability only affects products that are no longer supported by the maintainer.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in D-Link DIR-825 up to 3.00b32 allows for a buffer overflow via the AddPortMapping function in the miniupnpd component. This could enable an attacker within the local network to execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or disruption of network services. The exploit is publicly available, increasing the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Replace or upgrade the affected D-Link DIR-825 router with a newer, supported model that receives regular security updates.
Disable Universal Plug and Play (UPnP) on the router if it is not required for network functionality.
Implement network segmentation to isolate critical systems from devices potentially impacted by this vulnerability.

title: A vulnerability was identified in D-Link DIR-825 3.00b32. This affects the function NMBD_process...
description: A vulnerability was identified in D-Link DIR-825 3.00b32. This affects the function NMBD_process of the file sserver.c of the component nmbd. Such manipulation leads to buffer overflow. The attack can only be initiated within the local network. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. This vulnerability only affects products that are no longer supported by the maintainer.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in D-Link DIR-825 3.00b32 allows for a buffer overflow attack via the NMBD_process function, potentially leading to unauthorized code execution or denial of service. However, the attack requires local network access and affects unsupported products, limiting its scope.
recommended_actions: Replace or upgrade the affected D-Link DIR-825 router with a supported and updated model.
Restrict local network access to trusted devices and users to minimize exposure.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices.

title: CVE-2026-32955 - SD-330AC and AMC Manager provided by silex technology, Inc. contain a stack-based buffer overflow vu
description: SD-330AC and AMC Manager provided by silex technology, Inc. contain a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in processing the redirect URLs. Arbitrary code may be executed on the device.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in SD-330AC and AMC Manager could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code on the device, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or disruption of services.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available patches or firmware updates provided by silex technology, Inc. to address the vulnerability.
Restrict network access to the affected devices and ensure they are not exposed to the internet without proper security measures, such as VPNs or firewalls.
Conduct a security review of all devices running SD-330AC and AMC Manager to identify unauthorized changes or signs of compromise.
